column1: # DOLCHEO+ encoding TSV — schema per canon/governance/writing-conventions.md
# D, O, L, C rows (6 cols): type
# Tab-separated. Order: D/L/C first, O-open last.
# Session: 2026-05-29 bugbot review pass on PR #25

column2: A GitHub resolved thread is not proof the code was fixed — verify against code
column3: All 11 bugbot (cursor[bot]) threads on PR #25 showed isResolved=true, but two were still live in the code: AUDIO_GUIDANCE still claimed audio was passthrough and not deployed (false after the container path shipped), and the MCP f arg had no media_type cross-check (f=opus + media_type=image passed Zod then 400 at the parser). The other nine were genuinely fixed (getRandom pool routing; SSRF guard incl. IPv4-mapped IPv6 and manual per-hop redirect validation with -max_redirects 0; AudioOptions.f; Zod codec enum; audio echo default opus; duration on MISS). Mechanism: resolved means someone clicked the button, often auto on the next bot run; it does not track code state. Always re-verify each finding against current source.
column4: GraphQL reviewThreads on PR #25: 11/11 isResolved=true; code inspection found 2 unaddressed. The 9 fixed ones confirmed by reading the current source.
column5: Trust the resolved flag (rejected — it is not evidence of a fix).

column2: Independent concurrent fix collided on rebase — defer to the teammate commit
column3: While fixing the two live findings I had staged an equivalent change; a teammate pushed 0eff96a (correct audio guidance + validate f against media_type) at the same time, causing a rebase conflict in src/lib/mcp-tool.ts. Resolution: reset to the teammate commit and dropped my duplicate code rather than fight the conflict, after verifying theirs is correct and equivalent (their #11 guard lives in the worker tool handler returning a clear error, arguably better placed than my buildToolResponse throw). Only this governance record was kept. With frequent teammate pushes, re-fetch immediately before editing shared files to avoid duplicate work.
column4: 0eff96a verified: AUDIO_GUIDANCE corrected; worker.ts handler validates f via AUDIO_F/IMAGE_F. typecheck clean; 86 tests pass.
column5: Keep my duplicate fix and force-resolve the conflict (rejected — needless divergence; theirs is equivalent and already on the branch).

column4: Add a regression test for the MCP cross-type f guard
column5: The f-vs-media_type validation (0eff96a) lives inline in the worker.ts MCP tool handler and has no unit test, so a revert would pass CI silently. Add a test that a cross-type f (e.g. f=opus with media_type=image, and f=webp with media_type=audio) yields the validation error. Awkward only because the guard is in the handler rather than buildToolResponse; closes by exposing the check or testing via the handler.
